result.markdown:
---
title: "Quickstart"
subtitle: "Your first hwpapi script in under five minutes"
---

This page walks through the smallest useful hwpapi workflow: open a
document, change some text, inspect a collection, save to PDF. Every
code block below is marked `eval: false` because it drives the HWP COM
server — run the snippets against your own HWP install.

# 1. Connect to HWP

::: {#741941e8 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from hwpapi import App

app = App()             # launches HWP if needed, reuses if already running
print(app)              # repr — shows engine id and visible state
```
:::


Pass `is_visible=False` to keep the HWP window hidden (useful for
server scripts):

::: {#d1544314 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
app = App(is_visible=False)
```
:::


The context-manager form calls `close()` and `quit()` on your behalf:

::: {#037c4166 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from hwpapi import App

with App() as app:
    app.new()
    app.doc.insert_text("Hello from hwpapi\n")
    app.save_as("hello.hwp")
```
:::


# 2. Open or create a document

::: {#a2005a4b .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
app.open("report.hwp")                 # open an existing file
app.new()                              # create a blank document
app.save()                             # save in place
app.save_as("report_final.hwp")        # save as a new file
app.save_as("report.pdf")              # format is inferred from extension
app.close()                            # close the active document
app.quit()                             # tear down the COM engine
```
:::


# 3. Work with text via `app.doc`

Everything document-scoped lives under `app.doc` (a
[`Document`](../reference/Document.qmd) instance):

::: {#c529dd59 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# Full document text
print(app.doc.text)

# Replace entire text in place
app.doc.text = "Entirely new content."

# Insert at cursor
app.doc.insert_text("Inserted line\n")

# Navigate
app.doc.cursor.goto_page(3)
app.doc.cursor.in_table()              # → bool

# Copy / cut / paste / select / delete
app.doc.select_all()
selected = app.doc.get_selected_text()
```
:::


# 4. Collections are dict-like

Every collection under `app.doc.*` supports `[]`, `in`, `for`, `len`,
`names()`, `filter(...)`:

::: {#47584911 .cell execution_count=6}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# Fields (누름틀) — read and write by name
names = app.doc.fields.names()         # → list[str]
for name in names:
    print(name, "=", app.doc.fields[name].value)

app.doc.fields["author"] = "홍길동"
"author" in app.doc.fields             # → True
del app.doc.fields["author"]           # remove one
app.doc.fields.clear()                 # remove all

# Bookmarks
app.doc.bookmarks.add("chapter-1")
for bm in app.doc.bookmarks:
    print(bm.name)

# Tables (Phase 3 target)
tbl = app.doc.tables[0]                # first table
tbl.cell(1, 1).text = "Q1"
```
:::


# 5. Formatting via context scopes

The v1 `app.set_charshape(...)` / `app.charshape_scope(...)` methods
are gone — use the module-level scopes:

::: {#9b8a0180 .cell execution_count=7}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from hwpapi.context import charshape_scope, parashape_scope, styled_text

# Block — several operations share one style, then restore.
with charshape_scope(app, bold=True, size=14, color="#C0392B"):
    app.doc.insert_text("Heading\n")
    app.doc.insert_text("Still bold red\n")
# ← cursor style restored here

# Paragraph-level — alignment and line spacing.
with parashape_scope(app, align="center", line_spacing=180):
    app.doc.insert_text("Centered, 180% line spacing\n")

# One-shot — insert a styled phrase inline.
styled_text(app, "urgent", bold=True, color="#E74C3C")
```
:::


# 6. Friendly units

::: {#48bfd36c .cell execution_count=8}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from hwpapi import units as U

U.mm(210)       # → HWPUNIT
U.pt(12)        # → HWPUNIT
U.inch(8.27)    # → HWPUNIT (approx 210mm)

U.to_mm(59430)  # → ~210.0
U.to_pt(1200)   # → 12.0

U.parse("210mm")
U.parse("12pt")
U.parse("8.27in")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1}
```
59544
```
:::
:::


# 7. Escape hatch

When the facade doesn't expose what you need, drop into
[`hwpapi.low`](../guide/low-level-escape-hatch.qmd):

::: {#1aba5f64 .cell execution_count=9}
``` {.python .cell-code}
from hwpapi.low import actions, parametersets

# Raw action by name
charshape = actions._Action("CharShape")
charshape.pset.Bold = True
charshape.pset.Height = 1400           # 14pt in HWPUNIT (100/pt)
charshape.run()

# ParameterSet class catalogue
list(parametersets.__dict__.keys())[:5]
```
:::


You can also reach the raw COM handle via `app.api` and the engine via
`app.engine`.

# Next steps

- Need to convert a v1 script? → [Migration guide](migration-v1-to-v2.qmd)
- Want more worked examples? → [Recipes](../recipes/index.qmd)
- Looking for a specific class? → [Reference](../reference/index.qmd)
